unit_20 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_20

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 318 全部题目 7 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to
defineexpediteintegrity
define: standard unit headword | expedite: standard unit headword | integrity: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to ______ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to ______ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, B　manifest; reveal
groundmanifestundercut
ground: standard unit headword | manifest: standard unit headword | undercut: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to
presumereconcile
presume: standard unit headword | reconcile: standard unit headword

The Internet is an eternally unfinished monument to __________, a text that aspires to be as big and multifarious as the world it describes.The Internet is an eternally unfinished monument to __________, a text that aspires to be as big and multifarious as the world it describes.
答案：A　inclusion
promotionabstraction

#5 Sentence Equivalence

答案：A, B　discordance; contention
for allrenowned

#6 SE
Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often_______, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often_______, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.
答案：B, E　extolled; acclaimed
disputeacclaim

#7 Text Completion
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
congestionairoutrage
congestion: standard unit headword | outrage: standard unit headword
#8 Text Completion
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique

#10 Text Completion
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in

答案：D, F　repudiate; recant

答案：A　incontrovertible

But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　weave; interruptedness; strangeness
entity

The science community’s perennial lament over inadequate budgets has come to seem increasingly ______, because government support for science and engineering has never been greater.The science community’s perennial lament over inadequate budgets has come to seem increasingly ______, because government support for science and engineering has never been greater.
答案：E　disingenuous
perennial

答案：D, E　belie; mask

答案：D　insurmountable
insurmountable
insurmountable: standard unit headword

Despite the occasional (i)______ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)______ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)______ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)______ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from

答案：B, E　confined; circumscribed

#55 Text Completion
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce

答案：C, F　mockery; derision

#57 Text Completion
Scientists once said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i)______: there was an abundance of theories but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii)______ all theories but one.Scientists once said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i)______: there was an abundance of theories but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii)______ all theories but one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　nearly infinite; eliminated

Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called ______.Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called ______.
答案：A, C　encyclopedic; exhaustive
rambling
rambling: standard unit headword
#59 Text Completion
Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)______ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)______ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt
unkempt

The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright ______.The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright ______.
答案：A, C　antediluvian; archaic
flighty
flighty: standard unit headword
#61 Text Completion
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis

答案：A, C　wayward; errant

This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. Blank 2
Blank 3

For example, the (ii) ____________ Maxwell's equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.Many of the major developments in fundamental physics of the past century arose from (i) ____________ existing ideas. For example, the (ii) ____________ Maxwell's equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　overcoming contradictions between; incompatibility of

Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong-and, consequently, we are lacking in any _________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong-and, consequently, we are lacking in any _________ discussion on how they might go right.

The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) ____________ place to do business.Nineteenth-century England presents something of (i) __________ , at least to those who believe that (ii) ___________ best promotes business ethics. Granted, it would be (ii) to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. context: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
De Grey is openly unconcerned with the larger context of his work, and in his __________ he comes to reflect the best and worst of scientific practice: imagination detached from broader social and ethical considerations.De Grey is openly unconcerned with the larger context of his work, and in his __________ he comes to reflect the best and worst of scientific practice: imagination detached from broader social and ethical considerations.
答案：B　myopia
context
context: standard unit headword
#105 SE
For all the flexibility of mind and spirit yoga is supposed to bestow upon those who embrace it, some yoga students can be surprisingly in their approaches to the practice.For all the flexibility of mind and spirit yoga is supposed to bestow upon those who embrace it, some yoga students can be surprisingly in their approaches to the practice.
答案：B, E　dogmatic; orthodox
for all
for all: standard unit headword
#106 SE
Fairy shrimp, having evolved to live in one of nature’s most ________ environments, are able to capitalize on the highly temporary pools of water found in spring woodlands.Fairy shrimp, having evolved to live in one of nature’s most ________ environments, are able to capitalize on the highly temporary pools of water found in spring woodlands.
答案：A, F　transient; ephemeral
ephemeral
ephemeral: standard unit headword
#107 TC TRIPLE
Rosenfeld’s study, while treading over some relatively well-known ground, offers a (i)_______ perspective on its contours. For one, her treatment of Leonard Woolf’s life is serious and respectful, going some distance in (ii)_______ his portrayal as a baleful monster and as a relentlessly (iii)_______ influence on his wife Virginia.Rosenfeld’s study, while treading over some relatively well-known ground, offers a (i)_______ perspective on its contours. For one, her treatment of Leonard Woolf’s life is serious and respectful, going some distance in (ii)_______ his portrayal as a baleful monster and as a relentlessly (iii)_______ influence on his wife Virginia.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　fresh; revising; deleterious
ground
ground: standard unit headword

Intent on molding backward and agrarian Russia into a modern state, the architects of the Russian Revolution (i) _________ the transformative powers of science and technology. Their (ii) the manifestations of modern science--railroads, radio, airplanes, and so forth--was potently expressed in language that often bordered on the mystical and magical.Intent on molding backward and agrarian Russia into a modern state, the architects of the Russian Revolution (i) _________ the transformative powers of science and technology. Their (ii) the manifestations of modern science--railroads, radio, airplanes, and so forth--was potently expressed in language that often bordered on the mystical and magical.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　extolled; collective infatuation with.
dispute
dispute: standard unit headword
#109 TC SINGLE
As a genre that prizes authenticity over artifice, memoir puts a special pressure on literary style: if the memoirist’s language tends toward the highly lyrical, it can _____________ the memoirist’s claim to truth.As a genre that prizes authenticity over artifice, memoir puts a special pressure on literary style: if the memoirist’s language tends toward the highly lyrical, it can _____________ the memoirist’s claim to truth.
答案：C　hinder
expeditehinder

Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored
ground
ground: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
When an organism is functioning normally, excessive fat is stored in adipose tissue. Thus, the (i) ____________ of such tissue may be (ii) ____________: the fat is diverted to other organs, such as the liver and the heart, causing serious health problems.When an organism is functioning normally, excessive fat is stored in adipose tissue. Thus, the (i) ____________ of such tissue may be (ii) ____________: the fat is diverted to other organs, such as the liver and the heart, causing serious health problems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; hazardous

答案：A, F　short-lived; ephemeral
ephemeral
ephemeral: standard unit headword
#113 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify

When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.
答案：A　invulnerable

for all: standard unit headword

But she quickly resolved that she would not (ii) __________ her personal views merely for the sake of (iii) __________.Concerned that her new colleague would not approve of her political opinions, the reporter briefly considered (i) __________. But she quickly resolved that she would not (ii) __________ her personal views merely for the sake of (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　prevarication; suppress; concord

答案：B, C　expedite; facilitate

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　esoteric; parochial

答案：A, E　lacking; wanting

abstraction: standard unit headword
#119 Sentence Equivalence
It is not unusual for American education leaders to hold up another nation as a model for school reform: in the mid-nineteenth century, such figures ______ the professionalism and structure of the Prussian school system.It is not unusual for American education leaders to hold up another nation as a model for school reform: in the mid-nineteenth century, such figures ______ the professionalism and structure of the Prussian school system.
答案：C, E　hailed; acclaimed

acclaim: standard unit headword

The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.
答案：A　verisimilitude
abstraction
abstraction: standard unit headword
#121 TC TRIPLE
The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

答案：B, F　dissemblance; duplicity

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undertook; platitudinous

In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly ________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly ________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.
答案：B, E　static; unvarying

entity: standard unit headword

答案：E　venality

答案：B, C　proclivity toward; predilection for

In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) _________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) ________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) _________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　originality; avant-garde; abandon

By now, however, his statements have taken on a different tone, becoming more (ii)_______, and consequently they are perceived as being more directed at coalition building.

Blank 2

Blank 3

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.

Blank 2
Blank 3

#159 Text Completion
Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)______ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)______ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)______ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)______ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)______ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)______ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　presented; defining features of; propose
define
define: standard unit headword
#160 TC TRIPLE
It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude

presume: standard unit headword

答案：D　variegated

abstract: standard unit headword

答案：B　an inborn
inborn
inborn: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　sparse; paltry

#164 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
insurmountable
insurmountable: standard unit headword

答案：A　an aesthetic
pragmatic
pragmatic: standard unit headword

答案：E　plastic

Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________, even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________, even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging

答案：B, F　capricious; impulsive

答案：B, E　speculative; conjectural

#172 Text Completion
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
for all
for all: standard unit headword
#173 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
studied
studied: standard unit headword

However, water mill’s great capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)______ the money required to build the mill.In medieval Europe, water mills were more (i)______ than windmills. It is true that windmills could be built virtually everywhere, whereas water mills (ii)______. However, water mill’s great capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)______ the money required to build the mill.

Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious

#182 Text Completion
Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work. (i)______ to propose measures that might (ii)______ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii)______ , they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work. (i)______ to propose measures that might (ii)______ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii)______ , they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　forgoing opportunities; ameliorate; reticence

There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)______ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii) ______ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)______ .Some scientists speculate that changes in atmospheric methane were responsible for some abrupt global warming events in the past such as the one at the end of the geologic period known as the Younger Dryas. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)______ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii) ______ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unstable; fairly quickly destroyed; ephemeral

However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.

Blank 3

#213 Sentence Equivalence
Even if the story now seems a surprisingly innocuous overture to the author’s later, more fully developed narrations, it ______ some of the key traits of those bleaker tales.Even if the story now seems a surprisingly innocuous overture to the author’s later, more fully developed narrations, it ______ some of the key traits of those bleaker tales.
答案：D, F　anticipates; prefigures
undercut
undercut: standard unit headword
#214 Text Completion
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data
studied
studied: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　indifference; apathy

Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. 答案：C, D　experimental; innovative

acclaim: standard unit headword
#218 Text Completion
The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes
promotion
promotion: standard unit headword
#219 Sentence Equivalence
Benjamin Franklin’s reputation is so much one of approaching scientific investigation with common sense empiricism that it is somewhat startling to realize how ______ the great experimenter’s mentality truly was.Benjamin Franklin’s reputation is so much one of approaching scientific investigation with common sense empiricism that it is somewhat startling to realize how ______ the great experimenter’s mentality truly was.
答案：B, D　speculative; conjectural

pragmatic: standard unit headword
#220 Text Completion
Few studies have been published on ground-squirrel dispersal, and most of them have involved very small sample sizes, thus most statement regarding ground-squirrel dispersal must be considered ______.Few studies have been published on ground-squirrel dispersal, and most of them have involved very small sample sizes, thus most statement regarding ground-squirrel dispersal must be considered ______.
答案：E　conjectural

答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable

studied: standard unit headword
#222 Text Completion
Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　rich; problem; augmented

#223 Sentence Equivalence
Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.
答案：B, E　confound; perplex

People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)______ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)______ dialogue.People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)______ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)______ dialogue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　clandestine; open

As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty

#226 Text Completion
The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility
perennial
perennial: standard unit headword
#227 Sentence Equivalence
For parents, the pleasure of letting children choose which book to read aloud together is not always ______: I well remembered my inner groans when my child would constantly pick my least favorite book from the shelf.For parents, the pleasure of letting children choose which book to read aloud together is not always ______: I well remembered my inner groans when my child would constantly pick my least favorite book from the shelf.
答案：D, F　unalloyed; unqualified

The fact that science dealt in statistics and numbers, that its logic was probabilistic, meant that the domain of moral questions (ii)______ it: moral questions, for Tagore, required certainties, not probabilities.

答案：D, E　presage; portend

#230 Text Completion
The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative

答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility

In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering

#234 Text Completion
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces

spate: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　galvanize; animate

Several studies (i)______ the assumption that paper cups, because they were made of natural products, were more environmentally (ii)______ than cups made of plastic (polystyrene). Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.Several studies (i)______ the assumption that paper cups, because they were made of natural products, were more environmentally (ii)______ than cups made of plastic (polystyrene). Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　dispelled; friendly; costs

#237 Sentence Equivalence
Flash floods are common in desert regions and were widespread before the evolution of trees and woodland soils; in contrast, flash floods are ______ in woodlands, where floodwaters, impeded by trees, form ponds.Flash floods are common in desert regions and were widespread before the evolution of trees and woodland soils; in contrast, flash floods are ______ in woodlands, where floodwaters, impeded by trees, form ponds.
答案：E, F　mitigated; abated

overshadow: standard unit headword
#238 Text Completion
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
return
return: standard unit headword
#239 Sentence Equivalence
The difficulty of reforming electoral politics is not lack of the right tools but the need to put them into the hands of impartial agents: the goal should be to build capacity while ______ partisanship.The difficulty of reforming electoral politics is not lack of the right tools but the need to put them into the hands of impartial agents: the goal should be to build capacity while ______ partisanship.
答案：B, D　constraining; deterring

Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate

答案：D, E　alarm; unsettle

Williams finds the appearance of Whitman’s Leaves of Grass in 1855 nearly ______ given the immense disparity between Whitman’s earlier published works, which Williams finds dismal, and the consummate mastery of Leaves.Williams finds the appearance of Whitman’s Leaves of Grass in 1855 nearly ______ given the immense disparity between Whitman’s earlier published works, which Williams finds dismal, and the consummate mastery of Leaves.
答案：D, F　inexplicable; incomprehensible

#244 Text Completion
One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern

答案：B, F　conform to; square with

答案：A　deliberate

In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)______.The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)______, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)______.

Blank 2

#252 Text Completion
Scientists said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i)______ : there was an abundance of theories, but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii)______ all theories but one.Scientists said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i)______ : there was an abundance of theories, but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii)______ all theories but one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　nearly infinite; eliminated

答案：B, F　shortcoming; fault

#254 Text Completion
The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation
air
air: stem: target sense: manner/atmosphere or public expression

答案：E　vehement

#263 Text Completion
Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay
for all
for all: standard unit headword

One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event

That definition would not be (i)______ ; that is, it does not contradict the evidence. Its (ii)______ , however, renders it less (iii)______ than definitions based on astronomical criteria, such as orbit, size, and gravitation.The traditional set of nine planets could have been retained by defining a planet as any object that has the unique combination of characteristics of any one of the nine objects that were considered planets in the late twentieth century. That definition would not be (i)______ ; that is, it does not contradict the evidence. Blank 2

答案：C, D, I　false; arbitrariness; defensible

Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. #307 Text Completion
The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)______ and (ii)______ , prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)______ , or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)______ and (ii)______ , prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)______ , or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose
fungible
fungible: standard unit headword
#308 Text Completion
Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)______ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)______ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　a significant struggle; entails

#309 Text Completion
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
insurmountable
insurmountable: standard unit headword
#310 Text Completion
This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author’s choice of format, which is the (i)______ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)______ reception that academics—motivated partly by (iii)______ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards—generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author’s choice of format, which is the (i)______ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)______ reception that academics—motivated partly by (iii)______ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards—generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　inverse; scornful; snobbish elitism

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　valedictory; glowing

#312 Text Completion
Because scientific breakthroughs tend to occur when a prevailing theory's predictions start to fail, scientists sometimes find it (i)______ when a theory persists—as the standard model of particle physics has done—in making (ii)______ predictions.Because scientific breakthroughs tend to occur when a prevailing theory's predictions start to fail, scientists sometimes find it (i)______ when a theory persists—as the standard model of particle physics has done—in making (ii)______ predictions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　frustrating; correct

#313 Text Completion

答案：C　prestige

Frequently that time happened in a great classroom somewhere, in the hands of the one (ii)______ teacher we can each remember.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　exciting; consummate

#315 Text Completion
Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal ______ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal ______ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.
答案：B　bemoaned

#316 Text Completion
For many years Cole experienced (i)______ his professional circle. He was a (ii)______ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association’s incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)______ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.For many years Cole experienced (i)______ his professional circle. He was a (ii)______ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association’s incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)______ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　anonymity outside; leading; esoteric

While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)______ the increasingly (ii)______ challenges that nature presents. They can, however, make it more (iii)______ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)______ the increasingly (ii)______ challenges that nature presents. They can, however, make it more (iii)______ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging

答案：C　complacency

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　poisonous; naturally occurs in

答案：C　insensitive to

答案：B　ungainly

答案：C, F　punctuated; intermittent

答案：A, E　malodorous; noisome

Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)______. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains—even the air we breathe—seem the norm and therefore (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal
